difficultyBalancingGuideline.objective: Keep each category beginner-friendly but progressively challenging.
difficultyBalancingGuideline.operationalRule: For every 20 questions, aim for about 8 easy, 8 medium, and 4 hard.

futureExpansionStrategy: Add versioned yearly packs, for example current-affairs-2026-v1, without rewriting core categories.
Store questions with tags such as province, era, theme, and sourceReference for smarter filtering.
Introduce localization fields like question_ne and explanation_ne for multilingual delivery.
Run the validation script in CI to block duplicates and malformed options before deployment.
Track gameplay analytics such as question miss rate to rotate weak or outdated questions first.

questions.question: What is the official name of Nepal?
questions.explanation: Nepal is constitutionally defined as the Federal Democratic Republic of Nepal.

questions.question: What is the capital city of Nepal?
questions.explanation: Kathmandu is the political and administrative capital of Nepal.

questions.question: Which currency is used in Nepal?
questions.explanation: Nepal uses the Nepalese Rupee, abbreviated as NPR.

questions.question: What is the international calling code of Nepal?
questions.explanation: Nepal is internationally reached with the country code +977.

questions.question: Which time zone does Nepal follow?
questions.explanation: Nepal Standard Time is UTC+5 hours and 45 minutes.

questions.question: What is unique about the national flag of Nepal?
options: It is the only non-rectangular national flag
questions.correctAnswer: It is the only non-rectangular national flag
questions.explanation: Nepal has the only sovereign national flag that is not rectangular.

questions.question: What is the national flower of Nepal?
questions.explanation: Laliguras, a rhododendron species, is the national flower of Nepal.

questions.question: Which bird is the national bird of Nepal?
questions.explanation: Danphe, also called Himalayan Monal, is Nepals national bird.

questions.question: What is the highest mountain in Nepal?
questions.explanation: Mount Everest is the highest peak in Nepal and in the world.

questions.question: What is the internationally accepted elevation of Mount Everest after the latest Nepal-China measurement?
questions.explanation: Nepal and China jointly announced 8,848.86 meters as the official height.

questions.question: Approximately how large is Nepal in area?
questions.explanation: Nepals total area is about 147,516 square kilometers.

questions.question: How many provinces does Nepal have?
questions.explanation: Under the federal structure, Nepal is divided into seven provinces.

questions.question: How many districts are there in Nepal?
questions.explanation: Nepal is administratively divided into 77 districts.

questions.question: How many local levels are there in Nepal?
questions.explanation: Nepal has 753 local levels including metropolitan, municipalities, and rural municipalities.

questions.question: Nepal is located between which two countries?
questions.explanation: Nepal is landlocked and bordered by China to the north and India on other sides.

questions.question: What is the official language of Nepal at the federal level?
questions.explanation: Nepali in Devanagari script is the official language of Nepal.

questions.question: Which animal is recognized as the national animal of Nepal?
questions.explanation: The cow is officially the national animal of Nepal.

questions.question: Which top-level internet domain is assigned to Nepal?
questions.explanation: The country code top-level domain for Nepal is .np.

questions.question: At what fixed rate is the Nepalese Rupee pegged to the Indian Rupee?
questions.explanation: The Nepalese Rupee has long been pegged to the Indian Rupee at 1 INR to 1.60 NPR.

questions.question: Which day is the official weekly public holiday in Nepal?
questions.explanation: Saturday is the regular weekly public holiday in Nepal.

questions.question: Who is credited with the unification of modern Nepal?
questions.explanation: King Prithvi Narayan Shah led the campaign that unified many principalities into modern Nepal.

questions.question: Which treaty signed in 1816 defined much of modern Nepals boundary with British India?
questions.explanation: The 1816 Sugauli Treaty followed the Anglo-Nepal War and set key borders.

questions.question: In which year did the Kot Massacre occur, leading to Rana dominance?
questions.explanation: The Kot Massacre in 1846 enabled Jang Bahadur Rana to establish Rana rule.

questions.question: When did the Rana regime end in Nepal?
questions.explanation: The Rana oligarchy ended in 1951 after political movements and royal return.

questions.question: Who became Nepals first democratically elected Prime Minister in 1959?
questions.explanation: B. P. Koirala led the first elected government after the 1959 election.

questions.question: Which king introduced the partyless Panchayat system in 1960?
questions.explanation: King Mahendra dissolved parliament and introduced the Panchayat system.

questions.question: Which movement restored multiparty democracy in 1990?
questions.explanation: The Peoples Movement of 1990, called Jana Andolan I, restored multiparty democracy.

questions.question: In which year did the Maoist insurgency begin in Nepal?
questions.explanation: The armed insurgency began in 1996 and lasted a decade.

questions.question: Which agreement formally ended the decade-long armed conflict in 2006?
questions.explanation: The Comprehensive Peace Accord in 2006 officially ended the conflict.

questions.question: In which year was the monarchy abolished in Nepal?
questions.explanation: The first Constituent Assembly declared Nepal a republic in 2008.

questions.question: When was the first Constituent Assembly election held in Nepal?
questions.explanation: Nepal elected its first Constituent Assembly in 2008.

questions.question: Which king was the last monarch of Nepal?
questions.explanation: King Gyanendra was the last monarch before Nepal became a republic.

questions.question: On which date was the Constitution of Nepal promulgated?
questions.explanation: Nepal promulgated its current constitution on September 20, 2015.

questions.question: Which dynasty ruled Kathmandu Valley before the Shah conquest?
questions.explanation: The Malla kings ruled the valley city-states before unification.

questions.question: Which period is known for early inscriptions and development in the Kathmandu Valley before the Malla era?
questions.explanation: The Licchhavi period is noted for inscriptions, administration, and art.

questions.question: Which king returned from exile and symbolized the end of Rana absolute rule?
questions.explanation: King Tribhuvan returned in 1951 and became central to the political transition.

questions.question: Which event is commonly called Jana Andolan II?
questions.explanation: Jana Andolan II in 2006 led to restoration of parliament and major political change.

questions.question: What was the main outcome of the Anglo-Nepal War for Nepal?
options: Nepal signed the Sugauli Treaty and ceded territory
questions.correctAnswer: Nepal signed the Sugauli Treaty and ceded territory
questions.explanation: After the war, Nepal signed the Sugauli Treaty and lost territory to British India.

questions.question: Which year is associated with the first democratic general election in Nepal?
questions.explanation: Nepal held its first democratic general election in 1959.

questions.question: What happened on May 28, 2008, in Nepal?
options: The monarchy was abolished and Nepal became a republic
questions.correctAnswer: The monarchy was abolished and Nepal became a republic
questions.explanation: The Constituent Assembly declared Nepal a federal democratic republic.

questions.question: Which three broad physiographic regions define Nepal?
questions.explanation: Nepal is commonly divided into the Himalayan, Hilly, and Terai regions.

questions.question: Which is generally considered the longest river system in Nepal?
questions.explanation: The Karnali is the longest river system associated with Nepal.

questions.question: Which is the largest freshwater lake of Nepal by surface area?
questions.explanation: Rara Lake in Mugu is Nepals largest freshwater lake.

questions.question: Which valley is home to the capital city Kathmandu?
questions.explanation: Kathmandu city lies in the Kathmandu Valley.

questions.question: What is the lowest point of Nepal?
questions.explanation: Kechana Kalan in Jhapa district is often cited as Nepals lowest point.

questions.question: Which major river flows through Kathmandu?
questions.explanation: The Bagmati River flows through the Kathmandu Valley.

questions.question: In which district is Rara Lake located?
questions.explanation: Rara Lake lies within Mugu district in northwestern Nepal.

questions.question: Tilicho Lake is located in which district?
questions.explanation: Tilicho Lake is in Manang district, in the Annapurna region.

questions.question: Which is the only hunting reserve in Nepal?
questions.explanation: Dhorpatan Hunting Reserve is the only designated hunting reserve in Nepal.

questions.question: Which conservation area is the largest in Nepal?
questions.explanation: Annapurna Conservation Area is the largest protected area in Nepal.

questions.question: In which district is Shey Phoksundo National Park primarily located?
questions.explanation: Shey Phoksundo National Park lies mainly in Dolpa district.

questions.question: Which major highway runs east-west across Nepal?
questions.explanation: The East-West Highway is also known as the Mahendra Highway.

questions.question: Which river system is formed by seven tributaries and called Saptakoshi?
questions.explanation: Saptakoshi refers to the Koshi system formed by seven rivers.

questions.question: Mount Everest lies in which province of Nepal?
questions.explanation: Everest is located in Solukhumbu district, which is in Koshi Province.

questions.question: Phewa Lake is a major attraction of which city?
questions.explanation: Phewa Lake is one of the iconic landmarks of Pokhara.

questions.question: What is the approximate north-south width range of Nepal?
questions.explanation: Nepals north-south width varies roughly between 90 km and 241 km.

questions.question: Which statement best describes Nepals climate zones?
options: Climate ranges from tropical lowlands to alpine highlands
questions.correctAnswer: Climate ranges from tropical lowlands to alpine highlands
questions.explanation: Due to major elevation differences, Nepal has diverse climate zones.

questions.question: Which city is often called the gateway to the Annapurna region?
questions.explanation: Pokhara is a key tourism hub for Annapurna trekking routes.

questions.question: Which zone of Nepal contains the fertile plains most suitable for large-scale agriculture?
questions.explanation: The Terai plains are fertile and central to Nepals agricultural output.

questions.question: What is the approximate east-west length of Nepal?
questions.explanation: Nepal stretches roughly 885 km from east to west.

questions.question: Nepal is constitutionally defined as which type of state?
questions.explanation: The constitution defines Nepal as a federal democratic republican state.

questions.question: Who is the head of state in Nepal?
questions.explanation: In Nepal, the President serves as head of state.

questions.question: Who is the head of government in Nepal?
questions.explanation: The Prime Minister leads the government and executive at the federal level.

questions.question: How many members are elected to the House of Representatives?
questions.explanation: The House of Representatives has 275 seats in total.

questions.question: How many members are there in the National Assembly of Nepal?
questions.explanation: The upper house, National Assembly, has 59 members.

questions.question: What is the legal voting age in Nepal?
questions.explanation: Nepali citizens can vote in elections after turning 18.

questions.question: Which constitutional body conducts elections in Nepal?
questions.explanation: The Election Commission manages and supervises elections in Nepal.

questions.question: Which institution is primarily responsible for investigating corruption in public office?
questions.explanation: The Commission for the Investigation of Abuse of Authority handles corruption investigations.

questions.question: How often are local level elections generally held in Nepal?
questions.explanation: Local governments are elected for a five-year term.

questions.question: How many tiers of government does Nepal have under federalism?
questions.explanation: Nepal has federal, provincial, and local levels of government.

questions.question: Who heads the provincial government in Nepal?
questions.explanation: Each province has a Chief Minister as head of the provincial executive.

questions.question: Who presides over meetings of the House of Representatives?
questions.explanation: The Speaker chairs proceedings of the House of Representatives.

questions.question: Which court is the highest judicial authority in Nepal?
questions.explanation: The Supreme Court is the apex court in Nepal.

questions.question: How is the President of Nepal elected?
options: By an electoral college of federal and provincial lawmakers
questions.correctAnswer: By an electoral college of federal and provincial lawmakers
questions.explanation: The President is chosen by an electoral college of federal and provincial representatives.

questions.question: What is the term length of the House of Representatives, unless dissolved earlier?
questions.explanation: The House of Representatives normally serves a five-year term.

questions.question: Which statement about the National Assembly is correct?
options: It can be dissolved at any time
It is a permanent house with staggered terms
Its members are directly elected only by national popular vote
questions.correctAnswer: It is a permanent house with staggered terms
questions.explanation: The National Assembly is a continuing body with rotating member terms.

questions.question: What is the constitutional term of the President of Nepal?
questions.explanation: The Presidents term is five years under the constitution.

questions.question: How many provinces currently make up federal Nepal?
questions.explanation: Federal Nepal has seven provinces.

questions.question: Which office provides legal advice to the Government of Nepal?
questions.explanation: The Attorney General is the chief legal advisor to the government.

questions.question: A constitutional amendment in Nepal generally requires what level of federal parliamentary support?
questions.explanation: Constitutional amendments generally require a two-thirds majority of existing members in both houses.

questions.question: What is the official currency code of Nepalese Rupee?
questions.explanation: The currency code for Nepalese Rupee is NPR.

questions.question: Which institution functions as the central bank of Nepal?
questions.explanation: Nepal Rastra Bank is the central monetary authority.

questions.question: What is the name of the stock exchange of Nepal?
questions.explanation: Nepal Stock Exchange is abbreviated as NEPSE.

questions.question: Which country is Nepals largest trading partner?
questions.explanation: India is Nepals largest trading partner by overall volume.

questions.question: Which source contributes significantly to Nepals foreign exchange earnings?
questions.explanation: Remittances from Nepalis working abroad are a major source of foreign exchange.

questions.question: What is the standard Value Added Tax rate in Nepal?
questions.explanation: Nepals standard VAT rate is 13 percent.

questions.question: Which district is famous for high-quality orthodox tea production in Nepal?
questions.explanation: Ilam is widely known for tea cultivation.

questions.question: Which cash crop is Nepal internationally known for exporting in large quantity?
questions.explanation: Nepal is a notable exporter of large cardamom.

questions.question: Which sector generally employs a large share of Nepals workforce?
questions.explanation: Agriculture remains a major source of employment in Nepal.

questions.question: Which sector has become a major contributor to Nepals GDP in recent years?
questions.explanation: Services have become a key contributor to the economy.

questions.question: What is the installed capacity of Upper Tamakoshi Hydropower Project?
questions.explanation: Upper Tamakoshi is a major domestic hydropower project of 456 MW.

questions.question: Which inland trade gateway is one of the busiest for Nepals third-country cargo?
questions.explanation: Birgunj is a major logistics and customs gateway for trade.

questions.question: Which institution presents the annual national budget in federal parliament?
questions.explanation: The Finance Minister presents the annual budget in parliament.

questions.question: Which pattern has characterized Nepali labor migration for employment?
options: Mostly to Gulf countries and Malaysia
questions.correctAnswer: Mostly to Gulf countries and Malaysia
questions.explanation: A large share of foreign employment migration has gone to Gulf states and Malaysia.

questions.question: Which traditional export product helped build Nepals image in global textile markets?
questions.explanation: Nepali pashmina became well-known in international markets.

questions.question: Why is hydropower often called a strategic economic sector in Nepal?
options: Because Nepal has abundant river systems and gradient
Because Nepal has huge oil reserves
Because Nepal has many nuclear plants
Because hydropower needs no transmission lines
questions.correctAnswer: Because Nepal has abundant river systems and gradient
questions.explanation: Nepals rivers and terrain provide strong potential for hydropower generation.

questions.question: How do cooperatives contribute to Nepals economy?
options: By supporting savings, credit, and local enterprise
questions.correctAnswer: By supporting savings, credit, and local enterprise
questions.explanation: Cooperatives provide grassroots financial inclusion and local investment support.

questions.question: Which long-term UN development transition has Nepal targeted around 2026?
options: Graduation from Least Developed Country status
questions.correctAnswer: Graduation from Least Developed Country status
questions.explanation: Nepal has been preparing for graduation from LDC status on the UN timeline.

questions.question: Which tourism segment directly supports mountain-region local economies?
questions.explanation: Trekkers and climbers create income for guides, lodges, and transport services.

questions.question: What is one major macroeconomic risk for Nepal if remittance growth weakens sharply?
options: Lower pressure on foreign exchange reserves
Pressure on balance of payments and consumption
Automatic increase in domestic oil production
questions.correctAnswer: Pressure on balance of payments and consumption
questions.explanation: Remittances support both foreign exchange reserves and household consumption.

questions.question: Which religion has the largest number of followers in Nepal?
questions.explanation: Hinduism is the largest religion by population in Nepal.

questions.question: Lumbini is globally important because it is the birthplace of whom?
questions.explanation: Lumbini in Nepal is recognized as the birthplace of Siddhartha Gautama Buddha.

questions.question: Pashupatinath Temple is primarily dedicated to which deity?
questions.explanation: Pashupatinath is one of the most important Shiva temples in South Asia.

questions.question: Boudhanath is best known as what kind of religious monument?
questions.explanation: Boudhanath is one of the largest and most important Buddhist stupas in Nepal.

questions.question: What is the living goddess tradition of Kathmandu Valley called?
questions.explanation: The Kumari is a living goddess tradition practiced especially among Newar communities.

questions.question: Which phrase is the most common respectful greeting in Nepal?
questions.explanation: Namaste is widely used in Nepal as a respectful greeting.

questions.question: Which traditional social institution in Nepal historically managed land and religious trusts?
questions.explanation: Guthi institutions have long supported temples, festivals, and social works.

questions.question: Which river is especially associated with Hindu cremation ghats in Kathmandu?
questions.explanation: Important cremation rituals are performed on the banks of the Bagmati.

questions.question: Which musical instrument is widely associated with Nepali folk rhythms?
questions.explanation: The madal is one of the most commonly used percussion instruments in Nepali folk music.

questions.question: What is Paubha in Nepali cultural tradition?
questions.explanation: Paubha is a traditional Newar painting style with religious themes.

questions.question: Which architecture style is strongly associated with many historic Nepali temples?
questions.explanation: Multi-tiered pagoda architecture is a signature temple style in Nepal.

questions.question: Daura Suruwal is traditionally associated with which group?
questions.explanation: Daura Suruwal is commonly recognized as a traditional male attire in Nepal.

questions.question: Bratabandha in Hindu Nepali tradition is best described as what?
questions.explanation: Bratabandha is a coming-of-age sacred thread ceremony.

questions.question: Which practice was criminalized in Nepal due to harm against women, especially in parts of the Far-West?
questions.explanation: Chhaupadi was criminalized because it causes severe discrimination and health risks.

questions.question: Yomari is a traditional delicacy most strongly associated with which community?
questions.explanation: Yomari is a famous Newar festive food in the Kathmandu Valley.

questions.question: Kirat religious traditions in Nepal are often referred to by which term?
questions.explanation: Kirat communities preserve spiritual traditions through Mundhum teachings.

questions.question: Which city is especially known for Newar heritage architecture and cultural rituals?
questions.explanation: Kathmandu Valley cities are central to Newar culture and architecture.

questions.question: What is the cultural meaning of Tika and Jamara during Dashain?
options: Blessing from elders for protection and prosperity
questions.correctAnswer: Blessing from elders for protection and prosperity
questions.explanation: During Dashain, elders give Tika and Jamara as blessings.

questions.question: Which Nepali cultural value is reflected by touching the feet of elders in some communities?
questions.explanation: Touching elders feet symbolizes respect and receiving blessings.

questions.question: Why is Nepal often described as a country of cultural syncretism?
options: Because only one religion is legally allowed
Because Hindu and Buddhist traditions often coexist and overlap
questions.correctAnswer: Because Hindu and Buddhist traditions often coexist and overlap
questions.explanation: Many sites and practices in Nepal reflect shared Hindu-Buddhist cultural traditions.

questions.question: Which is widely considered the biggest festival in Nepal?
questions.explanation: Dashain is the longest and most widely celebrated festival in Nepal.

questions.question: Tihar is commonly known as what?
questions.explanation: Tihar is associated with lights, decorations, and worship over multiple days.

questions.question: Which day of Tihar celebrates the bond between brothers and sisters?
questions.explanation: Bhai Tika honors sibling relationships through ritual blessings.

questions.question: In Tihar, Kukur Tihar is the day dedicated to which animal?
questions.explanation: Kukur Tihar honors dogs with garlands and offerings.

questions.question: Which goddess is especially worshipped during Laxmi Puja in Tihar?
questions.explanation: Laxmi Puja is dedicated to the goddess of wealth and prosperity.

questions.question: Holi is also called what in Nepal?
questions.explanation: Holi is popularly called Fagu Purnima in Nepal.

questions.question: Teej is primarily associated with devotion to which deity?
questions.explanation: Many women observe fasting and prayers to Shiva during Teej.

questions.question: Chhath festival is mainly dedicated to the worship of what?
questions.explanation: Chhath worship centers on the Sun and Chhathi Maiya.

questions.question: Buddha Jayanti commemorates what?
questions.explanation: Buddha Jayanti marks the birth of Gautama Buddha.

questions.question: Which festival attracts large numbers of sadhus and devotees to Pashupatinath?
questions.explanation: Maha Shivaratri is one of the biggest observances at Pashupatinath.

questions.question: Indra Jatra is especially celebrated in which city?
questions.explanation: Indra Jatra is a major Kathmandu Valley festival with chariot processions.

questions.question: Bisket Jatra is most famously associated with which place?
questions.explanation: Bhaktapur is especially known for the dramatic Bisket Jatra celebrations.

questions.question: Gai Jatra originated as a procession connected to what social purpose?
options: Remembering family members who died in the previous year
questions.correctAnswer: Remembering family members who died in the previous year
questions.explanation: Gai Jatra supports communal mourning and social satire traditions.

questions.question: Janai Purnima is linked with which Hindu practice?
questions.explanation: On Janai Purnima, many Hindu men renew the sacred Janai thread.

questions.question: Maghe Sankranti marks what seasonal transition?
questions.explanation: Maghe Sankranti symbolizes the suns northward movement in Hindu tradition.

questions.question: Ghode Jatra is traditionally celebrated at which ground in Kathmandu?
questions.explanation: Horse races and displays of Ghode Jatra are centered at Tundikhel.

questions.question: Rato Machhindranath Jatra is centered in which city?
questions.explanation: Rato Machhindranath chariot festival is one of Patans most famous traditions.

questions.question: Losar in Nepal is primarily celebrated by which communities?
questions.explanation: Losar is observed by Tibetan and other Himalayan Buddhist groups.

questions.question: Udhauli and Ubhauli are major seasonal festivals of which indigenous groups?
questions.explanation: Kirat communities observe Udhauli and Ubhauli linked to seasonal cycles.

questions.question: What makes Nepali festivals socially significant beyond religion?
options: They strengthen family, community, and intergenerational ties
questions.correctAnswer: They strengthen family, community, and intergenerational ties
questions.explanation: Festivals in Nepal play major social roles in community bonding and identity.

questions.question: Which language is most commonly used for federal government records in Nepal?
questions.explanation: Nepali is the official language for federal government communication.

questions.question: Nepali language belongs to which language family?
questions.explanation: Nepali is an Indo-Aryan language using Devanagari script.

questions.question: Which script is primarily used to write Nepali language?
questions.explanation: The Nepali language is written mainly in Devanagari script.

questions.question: Maithili is widely spoken in which part of Nepal?
questions.explanation: Maithili is a major language in the southern plains, especially Madhesh areas.

questions.question: The Tharu people are historically associated with which region?
questions.explanation: Tharu communities are indigenous to many Terai areas.

questions.question: Which ethnic community is strongly associated with high-altitude mountaineering heritage?
questions.explanation: Sherpa communities are renowned for high-altitude knowledge and climbing support.

questions.question: Nepal Bhasa is traditionally the language of which community?
questions.explanation: Nepal Bhasa is the traditional language of the Newar people.

questions.question: Which script is associated with the Limbu language tradition?
questions.explanation: The Sirijanga script is associated with written Limbu language tradition.

questions.question: Bhojpuri speakers in Nepal are concentrated mainly in which belt?
questions.explanation: Bhojpuri is widely spoken in parts of the southern Terai region.

questions.question: Rai and Limbu communities are often linked with which broader indigenous identity in eastern Nepal?
questions.explanation: Rai and Limbu groups are often connected with Kirat cultural identity.

questions.question: Which statement best reflects language diversity in Nepal?
options: All local languages were replaced by English
questions.explanation: Nepal is one of the most linguistically diverse countries in South Asia.

questions.question: What is a key constitutional principle about mother tongues in Nepal?
options: Communities have rights to preserve and promote their languages
Only one local language can exist per province
They can be used only at home
questions.correctAnswer: Communities have rights to preserve and promote their languages
questions.explanation: The constitution recognizes cultural and linguistic rights of communities.

questions.question: Which community is widely known for villages around Annapurna and global Gurkha service history?
questions.explanation: Gurung communities are prominent in central hills and in military service history.

questions.question: Magar communities are historically significant in which broad region?
questions.explanation: Magar communities have strong historical presence in western hill regions.

questions.question: Thakali people are traditionally linked with which trans-Himalayan trade region?
questions.explanation: Thakali communities are strongly associated with the Mustang trade corridor.

questions.question: Which language is often used as a lingua franca across many Nepali ethnic groups?
questions.explanation: Nepali often serves as a common bridge language across communities.

questions.question: Which statement is true about ethnic diversity in Nepal?
options: Nepal has only one ethnic group
Nepal has dozens of officially recognized ethnic and caste communities
questions.correctAnswer: Nepal has dozens of officially recognized ethnic and caste communities
questions.explanation: Nepal has a highly diverse social composition across regions.

questions.question: Many Sherpa religious and cultural practices are closely tied to which tradition?
questions.explanation: Sherpa communities have deep historical links with Tibetan Buddhist traditions.

questions.question: Which annual observance in Nepal highlights the importance of mother tongues and linguistic rights?
questions.explanation: Nepal marks International Mother Language Day through many local programs.

questions.question: Why is multilingual education policy important for Nepal?
options: It helps inclusion and learning for children from diverse language backgrounds
questions.correctAnswer: It helps inclusion and learning for children from diverse language backgrounds
questions.explanation: Multilingual education improves access and outcomes in a linguistically diverse country.

questions.question: How many UNESCO World Heritage properties are listed in Nepal?
questions.explanation: Nepal has four UNESCO World Heritage properties: Kathmandu Valley, Lumbini, Chitwan, and Sagarmatha.

questions.question: Which UNESCO property in Nepal includes seven monumental zones?
questions.explanation: Kathmandu Valley World Heritage listing includes seven monument zones.

questions.question: Which site in Nepal is recognized as the birthplace of Buddha?
questions.explanation: Lumbini in Rupandehi is globally revered as Buddhas birthplace.

questions.question: Sagarmatha National Park is famous for containing which landmark?
questions.explanation: Sagarmatha National Park includes Mount Everest and surrounding Himalayan ecosystems.

questions.question: Chitwan National Park is especially known for conservation of which species?
questions.explanation: Chitwan is a flagship habitat for the greater one-horned rhinoceros.

questions.question: Pashupatinath Temple stands on the bank of which river?
questions.explanation: Pashupatinath is located on the sacred Bagmati River in Kathmandu.

questions.question: Boudhanath and Swayambhunath are best classified as what?
questions.explanation: Both are major Buddhist stupa complexes in Kathmandu Valley.

questions.question: Patan Durbar Square is located in which city?
questions.explanation: Patan Durbar Square is located in Lalitpur, also known as Patan.

questions.question: Which heritage square is famous for 55 Window Palace?
questions.explanation: The historic 55 Window Palace is a key attraction in Bhaktapur Durbar Square.

questions.question: Which city is the main gateway for Annapurna trekking routes?
questions.explanation: Most Annapurna region trekkers begin logistics from Pokhara.

questions.question: Everest Base Camp trekking is primarily associated with which region?
questions.explanation: Everest Base Camp lies in the Khumbu region of Solukhumbu.

questions.question: Which area of Nepal generally requires a restricted-area permit for foreign trekkers?
questions.explanation: Upper Mustang is regulated as a restricted trekking area.

questions.question: Janaki Temple, an important pilgrimage site, is located in which city?
questions.explanation: Janakpur is famous for Janaki Temple and Mithila heritage.

questions.question: Rara Lake tourism is most strongly associated with which district?
questions.explanation: Rara Lake lies in Mugu and is a major remote-ecotourism destination.

questions.question: Gautam Buddha International Airport is located near which city?
questions.explanation: The airport serves the Lumbini area from near Bhairahawa in Rupandehi.

questions.question: Pokhara International Airport was inaugurated in which year?
questions.explanation: Pokhara International Airport formally opened in 2023.

questions.question: Which adventure activity is Pokhara especially famous for?
questions.explanation: Pokhara is internationally known for tandem paragliding with mountain views.

questions.question: Which tourism model helps distribute income directly to rural mountain communities in Nepal?
questions.explanation: Community-based models channel tourism spending to local households.

questions.question: Why are heritage restoration efforts important after earthquakes in Nepal?
options: To preserve identity, faith spaces, and tourism livelihoods
questions.correctAnswer: To preserve identity, faith spaces, and tourism livelihoods
questions.explanation: Restoration protects culture and supports local economies linked to heritage tourism.

questions.question: Which UNESCO natural heritage site in Nepal is famous for both biodiversity and iconic mountain scenery?
questions.explanation: Sagarmatha combines globally significant mountain landscapes and biodiversity.

questions.question: How many of the worlds fourteen 8,000-meter peaks are in Nepal (fully or partly)?
questions.explanation: Nepal is associated with eight of the fourteen peaks above 8,000 meters.

questions.question: Which mountain is the highest in the world and in Nepal?
questions.explanation: Mount Everest is the highest peak on Earth.

questions.question: Which mountain is the third-highest in the world and lies on Nepals eastern border region?
questions.explanation: Kangchenjunga is the worlds third-highest mountain.

questions.question: Which peak is the fourth-highest mountain in the world and near Everest?
questions.explanation: Lhotse, adjacent to Everest, is the worlds fourth-highest mountain.

questions.question: Which mountain is known as the fifth-highest peak in the world?
questions.explanation: Makalu is ranked as the fifth-highest mountain globally.

questions.question: Which 8,000-meter peak is the sixth-highest mountain in the world?
questions.explanation: Cho Oyu is generally ranked sixth among the worlds highest peaks.

questions.question: Which mountain is the seventh-highest in the world?
questions.explanation: Dhaulagiri is globally ranked seventh by elevation.

questions.question: Which mountain is the eighth-highest peak in the world?
questions.explanation: Manaslu is the eighth-highest mountain in the world.

questions.question: Annapurna I is ranked what in global peak height order?
questions.explanation: Annapurna I is the tenth-highest mountain in the world.

questions.question: Which was the first 8,000-meter peak ever climbed by humans (1950)?
questions.explanation: Annapurna I was the first successfully climbed 8,000-meter mountain.

questions.question: Which animal is Nepals national bird and lives in mountain forests?
questions.explanation: Danphe (Himalayan monal) is Nepals national bird.

questions.question: Which endangered big cat is found in Nepals high Himalayan region?
questions.explanation: Snow leopards inhabit high alpine and trans-Himalayan landscapes.

questions.question: Which protected-area species is emblematic of lowland conservation success in Nepal?
questions.explanation: The greater one-horned rhino is a flagship species in Nepals lowlands.

questions.question: Red panda habitat in Nepal is typically found in what ecosystem?
questions.explanation: Red pandas rely on temperate forests with bamboo cover.

questions.question: Which is the largest conservation area of Nepal?
questions.explanation: Annapurna Conservation Area is the largest conservation area in Nepal.

questions.question: What does GLOF stand for in Himalayan hazard studies?
questions.explanation: GLOF is a major climate-related hazard in mountain regions.

questions.question: Monsoon rainfall in Nepal is primarily driven by moisture-bearing winds from which source?
questions.explanation: South Asian monsoon systems bring moisture from the Bay of Bengal.

questions.question: Koshi Tappu Wildlife Reserve is famous for conserving which rare wild bovine?
questions.explanation: Koshi Tappu is known for the endangered wild water buffalo population.

questions.question: Why is spring rhododendron bloom ecologically and culturally notable in Nepal hills?
options: It signals seasonal transition and supports mountain biodiversity tourism
questions.correctAnswer: It signals seasonal transition and supports mountain biodiversity tourism
questions.explanation: Rhododendron bloom seasons are linked with ecology, culture, and tourism.

questions.question: Which statement best explains Nepals extraordinary biodiversity density?
options: Huge altitude variation over short distance creates many habitats
questions.correctAnswer: Huge altitude variation over short distance creates many habitats
questions.explanation: Nepals rapid elevation shifts create tropical to alpine habitat gradients.

questions.question: What is the official national sport of Nepal?
questions.explanation: Volleyball was officially declared the national sport of Nepal.

questions.question: What is the governing body for cricket in Nepal?
questions.explanation: Cricket Association of Nepal, CAN, governs cricket administration.

questions.question: What is the governing body for football in Nepal?
questions.explanation: All Nepal Football Association, ANFA, runs football activities in Nepal.

questions.question: Which is the main national stadium in Kathmandu used for major football and multi-sport events?
questions.explanation: Dasarath Rangasala is a key national stadium in Kathmandu.

questions.question: In which year did Nepal obtain ODI status in international cricket?
questions.explanation: Nepal gained One Day International status in 2018.

questions.question: Nepal first played in the ICC Mens T20 World Cup in which year?
questions.explanation: Nepal made its first T20 World Cup appearance in 2014.

questions.question: Nepal qualified again for ICC Mens T20 World Cup for which edition?
questions.explanation: Nepal secured qualification for the 2024 T20 World Cup.

questions.question: Which player is widely known as a former captain who helped modernize Nepali cricket?
questions.explanation: Paras Khadka is a landmark figure in Nepali cricket leadership.

questions.question: What bowling style is Sandeep Lamichhane best known for?
questions.explanation: Sandeep Lamichhane is internationally recognized as a leg-spinner.

questions.question: Gaurika Singh represents Nepal in which sport?
questions.explanation: Gaurika Singh is one of Nepals prominent swimmers.

questions.question: In which year did Nepal first participate in the Olympic Games?
questions.explanation: Nepal first competed in the Olympics in 1964.

questions.question: How many Olympic medals has Nepal won so far?
questions.explanation: Nepal is still pursuing its first Olympic medal.

questions.question: The 13th South Asian Games were hosted by Nepal in which year?
questions.explanation: Nepal hosted the 13th South Asian Games in 2019.

questions.question: Which government body oversees broad sports development and infrastructure in Nepal?
questions.explanation: The National Sports Council is the apex state sports body.

questions.question: Martyrs Memorial A-Division League is associated with which sport?
questions.explanation: A-Division is the top domestic football league structure.

questions.question: Everest Premier League, when organized, is a franchise tournament in which sport?
questions.explanation: Everest Premier League is a domestic franchise-format cricket competition.

questions.question: Winning ACC Premier Cup 2023 helped Nepal qualify for which continental event?
questions.explanation: Nepal won ACC Premier Cup and qualified for the 2023 Asia Cup.

questions.question: What does ANFA stand for?
questions.explanation: ANFA expands to All Nepal Football Association.

questions.question: What does CAN stand for in Nepali cricket administration?
questions.explanation: CAN is the Cricket Association of Nepal.

questions.question: Why is mountain trail running gaining visibility in Nepal sports culture?
options: Because Nepal has no road races
Because diverse terrain and altitude make Nepal a natural endurance-sport destination
Because only foreign athletes can join
Because it replaced all team sports
questions.correctAnswer: Because diverse terrain and altitude make Nepal a natural endurance-sport destination
questions.explanation: Nepals geography supports endurance sports such as trail running and mountain racing.

questions.question: Which is the oldest university in Nepal?
questions.explanation: Tribhuvan University is the oldest and one of the largest universities in Nepal.

questions.question: In which year was Tribhuvan University established?
questions.explanation: Tribhuvan University was established in 1959.

questions.question: Kathmandu University was founded in which year?
questions.explanation: Kathmandu University was established in 1991.

questions.question: Purbanchal University was established in which year?
questions.explanation: Purbanchal University was founded in 1993.

questions.question: Pokhara University was established in which year?
questions.explanation: Pokhara University was established in 1997.

questions.question: Nepal Sanskrit University was formerly known by which name?
questions.explanation: Nepal Sanskrit University was earlier called Mahendra Sanskrit University.

questions.question: Agriculture and Forestry University is located in which district area?
questions.explanation: Agriculture and Forestry University has its central campus in Chitwan.

questions.question: Mid-West University is headquartered in which city?
questions.explanation: Mid-West University is based in Birendranagar, Surkhet.

questions.question: Far Western University is headquartered in which city?
questions.explanation: Far Western University is headquartered in Mahendranagar (Bhimdatta).

questions.question: Which university in Nepal is specifically designed to support open and distance learning?
questions.explanation: Nepal Open University focuses on flexible distance-based higher education.

questions.question: SEE in Nepal is generally taken at the completion of which grade?
questions.explanation: SEE is the major national examination after Grade 10.

questions.question: Which body manages school-level Grade 11 and 12 examinations in Nepal?
questions.explanation: The National Examination Board manages Grade 11 and 12 board examinations.

questions.question: Which institution focuses on technical and vocational education in Nepal?
questions.explanation: CTEVT is the main authority for technical and vocational training streams.

questions.question: What is the role of the University Grants Commission in Nepal?
options: Fund and support quality assurance in higher education
questions.correctAnswer: Fund and support quality assurance in higher education
questions.explanation: UGC supports financing, standards, and quality assurance in universities.

questions.question: Institute of Engineering in Nepal is a constituent institute of which university?
questions.explanation: The Institute of Engineering is a constituent body under Tribhuvan University.

questions.question: Institute of Medicine in Nepal is under which university?
questions.explanation: The Institute of Medicine is one of Tribhuvan Universitys key institutes.

questions.question: Publicly funded schools in Nepal are commonly referred to as what?
questions.explanation: Government-supported public schools are often called community schools.

questions.question: Which council regulates standards of medical practice and registration in Nepal?
questions.explanation: Nepal Medical Council sets professional standards and registration processes.

questions.question: A key challenge in Nepals school education system is often described as what?
options: Too few students in all cities
Inequality in quality between urban and remote schools
questions.correctAnswer: Inequality in quality between urban and remote schools
questions.explanation: Geographic and socioeconomic gaps affect educational equity across regions.

questions.question: Why is digital learning infrastructure becoming more important in Nepals education policy?
options: To improve access for remote learners and continuity during disruptions
To replace all textbooks in one year
questions.correctAnswer: To improve access for remote learners and continuity during disruptions
questions.explanation: Digital access helps bridge distance barriers and supports learning continuity.

questions.question: On which date was Nepals current constitution promulgated?
questions.explanation: The Constitution of Nepal came into force on September 20, 2015.

questions.question: In which year were the latest completed federal parliamentary elections held in Nepal?
questions.explanation: Nepal conducted federal parliamentary elections in November 2022.

questions.question: Local level elections were held across Nepal in which year?
questions.explanation: Nationwide local elections were held in May 2022.

questions.question: Province No. 1 was officially renamed to what in 2023?
questions.explanation: In 2023, Province No. 1 was officially named Koshi Province.

questions.question: In 2020, Nepal amended its map to include which disputed areas?
questions.explanation: Nepal updated its official map in 2020 to include these northwestern territories.

questions.question: Gautam Buddha International Airport was inaugurated in which year?
questions.explanation: GBIA near Lumbini was inaugurated in 2022.

questions.question: Pokhara International Airport began formal operation in which year?
questions.explanation: Pokhara International Airport was inaugurated in January 2023.

questions.question: Which cricket tournament did Nepal win in 2023 to enter Asia Cup?
questions.explanation: Nepal won ACC Premier Cup 2023 and qualified for Asia Cup.

questions.question: Nepal played in which major continental cricket event in 2023?
questions.explanation: Nepal returned to the Asia Cup in 2023.

questions.question: Nepal secured qualification for ICC Mens T20 World Cup 2024 through which route?
questions.explanation: Nepal qualified through the ICC Mens T20 World Cup Asia regional qualification pathway.

questions.question: A major earthquake struck Jajarkot and nearby areas in which year?
questions.explanation: A damaging earthquake hit Jajarkot and surrounding districts in November 2023.

questions.question: In which month and year did Nepals parliament ratify the MCC compact?
questions.explanation: Nepal parliament ratified the MCC compact in February 2022.

questions.question: The MCC Nepal Compact entered into force in which month and year?
questions.explanation: MCC implementation formally entered into force in August 2023.

questions.question: The first meeting of the newly elected House of Representatives after the 2022 election was held in which year?
questions.explanation: The new federal parliament convened in early 2023.

questions.question: Nepal hosted a major international investment summit in Kathmandu in which year?
questions.explanation: Nepal organized a high-level investment summit in 2024 in Kathmandu.

questions.question: Nepal has targeted graduation from Least Developed Country status around which year?
questions.explanation: UN transition planning has centered around 2026 for LDC graduation.

questions.question: In recent years Nepal has begun exporting more surplus monsoon electricity mainly to which neighboring country?
questions.explanation: Cross-border power trade with India has expanded during surplus generation periods.

questions.question: Nepal started its COVID-19 vaccination campaign in which year?
questions.explanation: Nepal launched COVID-19 vaccination in early 2021.

questions.question: Visit Nepal 2020 campaign was heavily disrupted mainly due to which global event?
questions.explanation: The global pandemic severely impacted tourism plans for 2020.

questions.question: Which election cycle in Nepal highlighted the rise of newer political forces including the Rastriya Swatantra Party?
questions.explanation: The 2022 federal election saw notable gains by newer parties including RSP.

questions.question: Nepals current constitution was promulgated on which date?
questions.explanation: The current Constitution of Nepal came into force on September 20, 2015.

questions.question: Under the constitution, Nepal is declared what kind of state?
questions.explanation: The constitution defines Nepal as federal, democratic, republican, and inclusive.

questions.question: What is Nepals constitutional position on religion?
questions.explanation: The constitution defines Nepal as a secular state.

questions.question: Where does sovereignty and state authority rest according to the constitution?
questions.explanation: The constitution states that sovereignty and state authority are vested in the people.

questions.question: Which right ensures equal protection and treatment under law in Nepal?
questions.explanation: The right to equality prohibits unjust discrimination.

questions.question: Nepals constitution explicitly prohibits which social practice?
questions.explanation: The constitution prohibits untouchability and related discriminatory practices.

questions.question: Which constitutional right allows citizens to seek court enforcement of fundamental rights?
questions.explanation: Citizens can approach courts to enforce fundamental rights.

questions.question: Which of the following is a recognized fundamental right in Nepal?
questions.explanation: Right to privacy is constitutionally protected in Nepal.

questions.question: Which environmental principle is included as a constitutional right in Nepal?
questions.explanation: Citizens have constitutional protection related to a clean environment.

questions.question: How many tiers of government are constitutionally recognized in Nepal?
questions.explanation: Federal, provincial, and local are the three constitutional tiers.

questions.question: What type of federal legislature does Nepal have?
questions.explanation: Federal parliament has two houses: House of Representatives and National Assembly.

questions.question: Which is the highest court under Nepals constitutional judiciary?
questions.explanation: The Supreme Court is the apex judicial body.

questions.question: A constitutional amendment generally requires which threshold in federal parliament?
questions.explanation: Constitutional amendments typically need a two-thirds majority in each house.

questions.question: At what age does a Nepali citizen become eligible to vote in elections?
questions.explanation: Citizens can vote from age 18.

questions.question: What is the legal minimum marriage age in Nepal?
questions.explanation: The legal minimum marriage age in Nepal is 20 years.

questions.question: Which statement about capital punishment in Nepal is correct?
options: It exists only in military law
It is used in anti-corruption cases
questions.explanation: Nepal does not practice capital punishment under its current legal framework.

questions.question: A state of emergency in Nepal is declared by whom on recommendation of the Council of Ministers?
questions.explanation: Emergency proclamation is made by the President on cabinet recommendation.

questions.question: Which set includes constitutional commissions in Nepal?
options: CIAA, Election Commission, Public Service Commission
questions.correctAnswer: CIAA, Election Commission, Public Service Commission
questions.explanation: These are among key constitutional bodies with specific mandates.

questions.question: Nepals modern Criminal Code and Civil Code came into force in which year?
questions.explanation: New Muluki Criminal and Civil Codes came into effect in 2018.

questions.question: Why is federalism legally significant in Nepal after 2015?
options: It constitutionally distributes powers across federal, provincial, and local levels
questions.correctAnswer: It constitutionally distributes powers across federal, provincial, and local levels
questions.explanation: Federalism is a constitutional mechanism for power sharing and inclusion.

questions.question: Who is known for unifying modern Nepal in the 18th century?
questions.explanation: Prithvi Narayan Shah led the unification campaign that formed modern Nepal.

questions.question: Who was Nepals first democratically elected Prime Minister?
questions.explanation: B. P. Koirala became the first elected Prime Minister in 1959.

questions.question: Which literary figure is honored with the title Mahakavi in Nepal?
questions.explanation: Laxmi Prasad Devkota is celebrated as Mahakavi in Nepali literature.

questions.question: Who is popularly known as Adikavi in Nepal?
questions.explanation: Bhanubhakta Acharya is widely known as the first poet of Nepali literature.

questions.question: Which 13th-century Nepali artist is known for work in Tibet and Yuan China?
questions.explanation: Araniko is famed for architecture and artistic work beyond Nepal.

questions.question: Who was the first Nepali woman to summit Mount Everest?
questions.explanation: Pasang Lhamu Sherpa summited Everest in 1993.

questions.question: Which Nepali mountaineer gained global fame for climbing all 14 eight-thousanders in about six months in 2019?
questions.explanation: Nirmal Purja set a record pace with Project Possible in 2019.

questions.question: Who founded Maiti Nepal to combat human trafficking?
questions.explanation: Anuradha Koirala is internationally recognized for anti-trafficking activism.

questions.question: Which social activist is known for supporting children of incarcerated parents through Early Childhood Development Center initiatives?
questions.explanation: Pushpa Basnet is known for work with children affected by parental imprisonment.

questions.question: Which energy-sector leader is widely credited for major reduction of load-shedding in Nepal?
questions.explanation: Kul Man Ghising is associated with Nepal Electricity Authority reforms and improved supply.

questions.question: Who became the first female President of Nepal?
questions.explanation: Bidya Devi Bhandari was Nepals first woman President.

questions.question: Who was the first female Chief Justice of Nepal?
questions.explanation: Sushila Karki became Nepals first female Chief Justice.

questions.question: Manisha Koirala is best known in which field?
questions.explanation: Manisha Koirala is a prominent actor known across South Asian cinema.

questions.question: Which cricketer served as a long-time national captain and later cricket administrator?
questions.explanation: Paras Khadka played major leadership roles on and off the field.

questions.question: Sandeep Lamichhane is internationally known in which sport?
questions.explanation: Sandeep Lamichhane is one of Nepals most recognized international cricketers.

questions.question: Gaurika Singh represented Nepal in Olympics in which sport?
questions.explanation: Gaurika Singh is a leading Nepali swimmer and Olympian.

questions.question: Which singer, born in Nepal, became famous across South Asian playback music?
questions.explanation: Udit Narayan, born in Nepal, became a major playback singer in South Asia.

questions.question: Which Nepali author wrote the acclaimed autobiographical work Jiwan Kanda Ki Phool?
questions.explanation: Jhamak Ghimire is an acclaimed Nepali writer known for this autobiographical work.

questions.question: Madan Krishna Shrestha is most famous for contributions in which domain?
questions.explanation: Madan Krishna Shrestha is a major figure in Nepali theater and comedy.

questions.question: Hari Bansha Acharya is best known as part of which iconic Nepali comedy duo?
questions.explanation: Hari Bansha Acharya and Madan Krishna Shrestha are famous as MaHa Jodi.
